segments.text: Then I looked up, and there before me was a man with a measuring line in his hand.
To measure Jerusalem, to find out how wide and how long it is.
segments.speaker: Man with measuring line

segments.text: While the angel who was speaking to me was leaving, another angel came to meet him
Run, tell that young man, 'Jerusalem will be a city without walls because of the great number of people and animals in it.
segments.speaker: Angel

segments.text: And I myself will be a wall of fire around it, and I will be its glory within.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Come! Come! Flee from the land of the north, for I have scattered you to the four winds of heaven.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Come, Zion! Escape, you who live in Daughter Babylon!
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: After the Glorious One has sent me against the nations that have plundered you—for whoever touches you touches the apple of his eye—
segments.speaker: The Lord Almighty

segments.text: I will surely raise my hand against them so that their slaves will plunder them. Then you will know that the Lord Almighty has sent me.
segments.speaker: The Lord Almighty

segments.text: Shout and be glad, Daughter Zion. For I am coming, and I will live among you.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Many nations will be joined with the Lord in that day and will become my people. I will live among you and you will know that the Lord Almighty has sent me to you.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: The Lord will inherit Judah as his portion in the holy land and will again choose Jerusalem.
Be still before the Lord, all mankind, because he has roused himself from his holy dwelling.
